channel.title: Philosophy on A fractal spectrum of tales
channel.description: Recent content in Philosophy on A fractal spectrum of tales

item.title: Mathematics, Beauty and the Shadow of AI
item.description: There is this interesting stereotype that mathematicians deal with the cold, hard truth. Yet, even the most technical mathematics, deep down, is highly warm and playful. Most mathematicians long for the moment a proof finally clicks. This is something beyond its correctness: when it clicks, it also feels right and looks right. It usually comes with a sense of elegance or beauty, be it because of some surprising connection or for remarkable simplicity. It is common that in those cases one wonders—after the fact and forgetting the long struggle that led there—how come no one has seen it before.

item.title: Circular Time
item.description:
’Forty-two,’ said Deep Thought, with infinite majesty and calm. – Douglas Adams, The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy

The narrative on AI keeps swinging between panicked existential threats and overoptimistic hype. Last week, while flu forcefully made sure I sayed in bed, I had time to read some articles in my backlog and really found it disappointing how the discussion has stagnated and keeps revolving around the same points.

None of the ideas look anywhere new. In The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy, Douglas Adams already brilliantly satirized on the quest for superintelligence and what will happen with it.
